testmod.signature: doctest.testmod(m=None, name=None, globs=None, verbose=None, report=True, optionflags=0, extraglobs=None, raise_on_error=False) -> TestResults
testmod.description: Tests the interactive examples in the docstrings of all functions, classes, and the module itself within the given module (or __main__ by default). This is the primary entry point for running doctests in a module and returns a TestResults namedtuple with (failed, attempted) counts.

testfile.signature: doctest.testfile(filename, module_relative=True, name=None, package=None, globs=None, verbose=None, report=True, optionflags=0, extraglobs=None, raise_on_error=False, parser=DocTestParser(), encoding=None) -> TestResults
testfile.description: Tests the interactive examples in a text file containing doctest-formatted examples. The file is read and its examples are executed in a clean namespace. Returns a TestResults namedtuple indicating how many tests were attempted and how many failed.

run_docstring_examples.signature: doctest.run_docstring_examples(f, globs, verbose=False, name='NoName', compileflags=None, optionflags=0) -> None
run_docstring_examples.description: Tests the interactive examples associated with a single object's docstring. Useful for running doctests on a specific function or class without testing the entire module. The globs argument provides the namespace for evaluating the examples.

DocTestParser.description: Parses a string (typically a docstring) into a DocTest object containing a list of Example objects. The parser identifies interactive Python examples by looking for '>>>' prompts and groups them with their expected output. Handles continuation lines ('...') and interleaved explanatory text.

get_doctest.signature: parser.get_doctest(string, globs, name, filename, lineno) -> DocTest
get_doctest.description: Parses the given string and returns a DocTest object with the extracted examples, ready for execution.

get_examples.description: Extracts all Example objects from the string, returning them as a list without creating a full DocTest object.

parse.description: Divides the string into a list of alternating text chunks and Example objects, preserving all content.

DocTestRunner.description: Executes the examples in a DocTest object and reports the results. The runner compares actual output against expected output using an OutputChecker and tracks pass/fail statistics. Supports verbose mode for detailed reporting and custom option flags for controlling comparison behavior.

run.description: Runs the examples in the given DocTest, returning TestResults with (failed, attempted) counts.

summarize.description: Prints a summary of all test cases that have been run by this runner, including pass/fail totals.

merge.description: Merges the results from another DocTestRunner into this one, combining the test statistics.

DocTestFinder.description: Finds all docstrings in a module and extracts DocTest objects from them. Recursively explores classes, functions, methods, properties, and nested objects to find all testable docstrings. The exclude_empty option skips objects whose docstrings contain no examples.

find.description: Returns a list of DocTest objects found by examining the docstrings of obj and its contained objects recursively.

Example.signature: class doctest.Example(source, want, exc_msg=None, lineno=0, indent=0, options=None)
Example.description: Represents a single interactive Python example consisting of a source code statement (the part after '>>>') and its expected output (the lines following the source before the next '>>>' or blank line). Examples are the atomic unit of doctest testing.

DocTest.signature: class doctest.DocTest(examples, globs, name, filename, lineno, docstring)
DocTest.description: A collection of related Example objects extracted from a single docstring, along with metadata about where it was found. DocTest objects are created by DocTestParser and executed by DocTestRunner to verify that code examples produce their documented output.

ELLIPSIS.description: An option flag that allows '...' in expected output to match any substring in the actual output. This is invaluable for writing doctests with output containing memory addresses, timestamps, or other variable content that changes between runs.

NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE.description: An option flag that makes all whitespace sequences (spaces, tabs, newlines) compare as equal when checking expected versus actual output. Useful when exact formatting may vary across platforms or Python versions.
